key.name: show-quick-settings
key.description: Whether to show the Service Pauser toggle in GNOME Quick Settings.

key.name: auto-resume-on-disable
key.description: Whether to resume managed paused services when the extension is disabled.

key.name: refresh-interval
key.description: How often the extension refreshes service status, in seconds.

key.name: gsettings-targets
key.summary: GSettings switches to pause alongside services
key.description: JSON list of GSettings targets (schema, key, pause value) toggled together with the pause button, in addition to systemd units. Each entry may also hide its own extension's Quick Settings toggle while managed here. Folder Size (foldersize@yurij.de) moved to the file-targets key since it no longer exposes a GSettings schema.

key.name: gsettings-runtime-state
key.summary: Saved values for managed GSettings switches
key.description: Internal state used to restore GSettings switches to the values they had before Service Pauser changed them.

key.name: file-targets
key.summary: Config file switches to pause alongside services
key.description: JSON list of simple INI config file targets (path, section, key, pause value) toggled together with the pause button, for applications that store their state in a plain file instead of GSettings.

key.name: file-runtime-state
key.summary: Saved values for managed config file switches
key.description: Internal state used to restore config file switches to the values they had before Service Pauser changed them.

key.name: app-targets
key.summary: Desktop apps to stop alongside services
key.description: JSON list of Flatpak or Snap desktop app targets stopped together with the pause button. Snap daemons remain normal systemd service entries.

key.name: app-runtime-state
key.summary: Saved state for managed desktop apps
key.description: Internal state used to restart desktop apps only when Service Pauser stopped them while pausing.
